title: Jasminum noldeanum
taxonomy: Jasminum noldeanum is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae, commonly known as the olive family. It belongs to the genus Jasminum, which comprises a group of fragrant-flowering climbing plants. The plant is native to the Himalayas and is often cultivated for its showy white flowers and fragrant scent. Jasminum noldeanum is a flowering plant that has a classification of Jasminum noldeanum. The plant's classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida.
benefits: Jasminum noldeanum, a fragrant climbing vine, is said to have several health benefits. Its flowers and leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. The plant is also believed to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it useful in wound healing and skin care. However, more research is needed to confirm the full extent of its health benefits.
properties: Jasminum noldeanum is known for its potential therapeutic properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. constituents: Jasminum noldeanum contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal and therapeutic properties. preparations: Jasminum noldeanum, also known as Nolde's jasmine, is a type of fragrant vine native to China. intro: Jasminum noldeanum, commonly known as the Himalayan jasmine, is a flowering vine native to the Himalayan region. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Jasminum noldeanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
